title: Decadent Mexican Queso Quesadilla
description: A sweet, cheese-pull one-pot Mexican queso that melts into a creamy dip or sauce, featuring epoisses, dashi extract, and low-glycemic sweetener.
instructions: Heat a large saucepan over medium heat and add the dashi extract, coconut milk, and monk fruit sweetener, stirring until the sweetener dissolves.
Add the diced red onion and minced jalapeño, cooking until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the epoisses and season with cumin, chili powder, and salt, letting the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and fold in the shredded mozzarella, cheddar, and softened cream cheese until all cheeses melt into a smooth, pullable texture.
Once the cheese has fully melted, remove the pot from heat and let it rest for 2 minutes to thicken slightly.
Serve the queso hot in a shallow dish, optionally topping with fresh cilantro or sliced avocado.
This one-pot queso can be used as a dip, poured over tortilla chips, or spooned onto warm tortillas for a quick quesadilla.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days; reheat gently on low heat or in a microwave until warm.
